dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c Salt b Duffy 11 (9b 2x4 0x6 17m) SR: 122.22

dismissalComment.html: Head's snipered it straight to deep square leg! The crowd erupts! Duffy turning it on at the Chinnaswamy. 136ks back of length ball just outside off, Head pulled it hard and flat. Salt took it with cupped hands around his chest and fell back with the momentum but held on. Travishek done for the night

dismissalText.commentary: Ishan Kishan c Salt b Abhinandan Singh 80 (38b 8x4 5x6 73m) SR: 210.52

dismissalComment.html: Hang on a minute, I need to pick up my jaw from the floor. PHIL SALT, where's your cape! How's he taken that? A one-handed stunner right at the deep backward point boundary to dismiss the SRH captain. If there was any doubt about Salt's previous catch, there is none this time. Low full toss outside off stump, Kishan carves that again this time over point. The ball was going to the fence for all money but Salt hares to his right around the deep backward point region, plucks out his right hand, almost parallel to the ground and grabs it with minimum of fuss. WOWZA!

dismissalText.commentary: Heinrich Klaasen c Salt b Shepherd 31 (22b 2x4 1x6 42m) SR: 140.9

dismissalComment.html: Has Salt taken a screamer at the deep midwicket fence? Salt likes it but he was very close to the boundary cushion. Slower offcutter on off stump, Klaasen looks to mow that across the line, but courtesy of the lack of pace, gets more height than distance. Salt is right on the boundary edge and makes the catch more difficult than it is. The ball bobbles in his hand, and he ends up swatting on the boundary edge and completes the catch. The third umpire is checking, waiting for multiple angles to check if any part of Salt's body or his shirt was touching the boundary cushions. He is very close to those cushions... but looks like he is fine. The third umpire is happy and sends Klaasen on his way. This was too close for comfort, but I think the third umpire got this decision right. Salt actually showed great presence of mind to not tumble too close to the cushion

dismissalComment.html: Hang on a minute, I need to pick up my jaw from the floor. PHIL SALT, where's your cape! How's he taken that? A one-handed stunner right at the deep backward point boundary to dismiss the SRH captain. If there was any doubt about Salt's previous catch, there is none this time. Low full toss outside off stump, Kishan carves that again this time over point. The ball was going to the fence for all money but Salt hares to his right around the deep backward point region, plucks out his right hand, almost parallel to the ground and grabs it with minimum of fuss. WOWZA!

headshotImage.longCaption: Heinrich Klaasen player page headshot cutout, 2021

dismissalComment.html: Has Salt taken a screamer at the deep midwicket fence? Salt likes it but he was very close to the boundary cushion. Slower offcutter on off stump, Klaasen looks to mow that across the line, but courtesy of the lack of pace, gets more height than distance. Salt is right on the boundary edge and makes the catch more difficult than it is. The ball bobbles in his hand, and he ends up swatting on the boundary edge and completes the catch. The third umpire is checking, waiting for multiple angles to check if any part of Salt's body or his shirt was touching the boundary cushions. He is very close to those cushions... but looks like he is fine. The third umpire is happy and sends Klaasen on his way. This was too close for comfort, but I think the third umpire got this decision right. Salt actually showed great presence of mind to not tumble too close to the cushion
